page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of average and weak results, largely influenced by the shared CPU model. While single-core stress-ng performance falls into the bottom 10% and multi-core stress-ng is in the bottom 25%, Geekbench scores present average results with a single-core score of 1282.0 and a multi-core score of 6083.0. Memory performance is constrained, with PassMark memory scores and uncached large-block read/write bandwidths landing in the bottom 25%. However, cached small-block read bandwidth remains average at 825.47 GB/sec.

This server offers a budget-friendly option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. It is suitable for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, and basic Redis operations, where it achieves an average of 11.14 million operations per second. It is less suited for high-throughput web serving or medium-to-large language model inference due to its shared resource model and weak large-block memory bandwidth.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz. Operating under a shared CPU allocation model, this server delivers a balance of resource density and budget efficiency, though it exhibits clear performance tradeoffs. Benchmarks indicate average performance in Geekbench and Redis operations, but weaker results in stress-ng CPU tests and uncached memory bandwidth. With no local storage or bundled GPUs, this instance is designed for workloads that benefit from multiple cores without requiring dedicated CPU execution. It is best suited for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering a budget-friendly configuration for multi-threaded tasks with moderate CPU demands.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, designed for budget-conscious multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, multi-core virtual server with shared AMD EPYC resources, designed for non-intensive parallel processing and basic utility workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs with 16.0 GB of system memory.
KVM hypervisor architecture with no local storage or bundled GPUs.
Average performance in software compilation and Redis operations.
Reduced performance in uncached memory bandwidth and stress-ng tests.
Cost-effective option for non-intensive parallel processing tasks.
